page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 processors. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this server features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz. It is equipped with 192 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

In terms of performance, the instance exhibits a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks is categorized as poor, multi-core performance is average. However, the system excels in specialized workloads, achieving top-tier performance in PassMark CPU encryption tests and strong results in PassMark CPU physics, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large-block read and write speeds remain average.

The inclusion of 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance highly suitable for data-intensive operations. This is reflected in strong benchmark results for PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations. The hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency by combining high-density compute resources with fast local storage, reducing the need for external network-attached storage.

Workload fit for the c5ad.24xlarge includes multi-threaded applications, software compilation, cryptographic processing, database hosting, and medium-scale LLM inference. The lack of AVX-512 support is a notable architectural tradeoff for certain vector-heavy scientific computing tasks, but the presence of AVX, FMA, and SHA_NI instructions supports a wide range of standard enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It includes 192 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core benchmarks are relatively weak, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, alongside top-tier cryptographic execution. The combination of high-density compute and fast local storage provides a cost-efficient profile for resource-intensive workloads without relying on external storage. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and cryptographic processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded and database workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute instance featuring fast local NVMe storage and strong multi-threaded performance for database and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 at 3.3 GHz
192 GB DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in cryptographic encryption and strong database operations
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database hosting
